message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** A bot-generated documentation gap issue for `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` requesting updates to README troubleshooting content and the handler-implementation-guide to reflect new eager pre-handler validation behavior and structured error field propagation introduced in PR #58341.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`
**Affected API:** README.md (Troubleshooting section), `docs/handler-implementation-guide.md` (Handler Exceptions table + Validation Pipeline section)
**Scenarios:**
- Users expect `previous_response_id` / `conversation.id` validation to be silent or handler-handled, but it now returns HTTP 404/400 before the handler is invoked
- `ResourceNotFoundException` now carries `Code` and `Param` properties (matching `BadRequestException`), but docs still show `error.code: null`
- Non-400/404 upstream Foundry storage errors are normalized to HTTP 500, but this is not documented

**Root ask:** Update README troubleshooting section and handler-implementation-guide to document the new pre-handler validation and structured error fields.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only gap — no code behavior changes are needed. The implementation guide in the issue body provides precise file locations and diff content for both changes.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review PR #58341 (commit 16a9c8b7e752cd0375e7770aa2b0447d3b7193b0) to confirm the behavioral changes described
2. Update `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` Troubleshooting section with pre-handler validation errors and 500-normalization note
3. Update `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/docs/handler-implementation-guide.md` Handler Exceptions table and Validation Pipeline section to reflect `Code`/`Param` on `ResourceNotFoundException`
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — The issue is about SDK documentation and behavior, not REST API or service-side issues; not a management or provisioning package
- **Service:** `AI Agents` — Package is `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` in `sdk/agentserver/`; confirmed by prior issues #58268 and #58293 using the same label pair
- **Confidence:** High — Package name and service directory unambiguously map to a single service label; category is clearly "Client"
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %AI Agents` (line 85) — matched because predicted service label is "AI Agents"
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** @ankitbko @RaviPidaparthi
- **Routing action:** Added "Service Attention" label; issue is bot-filed (not customer-reported) so "needs-team-attention" was not added; @mentioned ServiceOwners via routing comment
- **Scan notes:** Scanned bottom-to-top from end of CODEOWNERS; no multi-label entries involving "AI Agents" were found; matched at line 85 on first relevant `%AI Agents` entry
</details>
